14.reason: Missing return statement for the case when root is NULL; the function should return the newly created node p.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing return statement for the case when root is NULL; the function should return the newly created node p.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing return p; when root is NULL, the function does not return the new node, causing undefined behavior.

21.reason: Missing return statement: function should return a pointer (struct BstNode *).

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing return statement for the non-NULL cases; the function should return root after insertion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing return root; at the end of the function, so the function does not return a value for non-NULL cases, causing undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing return root; at the end of function. The function must return the (possibly modified) root pointer.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing return statement: function should return a pointer (struct BstNode *).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing 'return root;' statement at the end of the function, which causes a compilation error for a non-void function.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing 'return root;' statement here before the function ends, causing a compilation error because the function must return a pointer value.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  // Missing return statement; function does not return a value in all paths, causing undefined behavior and likely compilation warning/error in strict mode.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  // Missing return statement; function does not return the root node in non-NULL cases, leading to undefined behavior.

13.reason: Missing return p; after allocating new node, function should return p to parent caller.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing return p; after allocating new node, function should return p to parent caller.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing 'return p;' statement, causing the function to not return the newly allocated node.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing 'return p;' statement here, causing a compilation error as the non-void function does not return the newly allocated node.
